rationale: My two round-2 blockers are addressed in 631d7c14. The target input path now has an explicit branch-name-safe grammar, rejects control bytes and leading-dash flag injection, is passed as its own argv-safe token, and has dedicated pre-registration e2e cases for metacharacter/argument injection and control bytes. The malformed-once proof is also now runnable: the first payload is narrative-wrapped but contains one schema-valid verdict object, while the second payload is a clean standalone reserialize, so CCR2's canonical comparison over verdict, hard_block, and concrete_changes_required has a real operand. I did not find a remaining G2-blocking input path.

findings.ask: G2.1 buildCommand chokepoint plus positive-enumeration structural test
findings.details: Carried forward from round 2 as addressed: the workflow routes commands through buildCommand, keeps ALLOWED_CLI to the exact four prefixes, pins codex read-only, and tests the exact set plus command-bearing literal inventory.

findings.ask: G2.2 input/path hardening contract plus e2e scenario 7
findings.details: The prior target gap is closed: target now uses a branch-name-safe grammar covering git check-ref-format disallowed constructs, control bytes, whitespace, and leading dash; the plan requires it to be passed as one argv-safe token; scenario 7 now separately covers slug traversal, target flag/metacharacter injection, and target control bytes before registration.

findings.ask: G2.3 exactly-one-JSON-object stdout parsing with fail-closed ambiguity and canonical-decode value fidelity
findings.details: The malformed-once e2e now specifies exact comparable payloads: first a narrative-wrapped schema-valid verdict object rejected by the one-object rule but extractable for canonical comparison, then a clean standalone reserialize. The fidelity check over verdict, hard_block, and concrete_changes_required is now executable.
